name: George Bluth Sr.
relation: Patriarch; founder of the Bluth Company
notes: Jailed in the pilot for 'light treason' (building houses for Saddam Hussein in Iraq). Identical twin of Oscar. Serially escapes custody: fakes a heart attack (s01e22), flees to Mexico (s02), hides in the model home attic (s02), house arrest (s03). Invented the Cornballer, banned in every civilized country.

name: Lucille Bluth
notes: Socialite, penthouse at Balboa Towers. Rivalry with Lucille Austero ('Lucille 2'). Winks conspiratorially; cannot judge the price of a banana ('It's one banana, Michael. What could it cost, ten dollars?'). Convicted of ordering the Queen Mary theft; later runs the family from prison and a beach cottage.

name: Michael Bluth
relation: Second-oldest son; the one holding the family together
notes: Widower — wife Tracey died of ovarian cancer (pre-series, 2001). Twin of Lindsay (she was adopted; born to be her twin). Runs the Bluth Company after George Sr.'s arrest. Repeatedly almost leaves for Phoenix. In s04–s05: fails to sell Sudden Valley houses, moves into George Michael's dorm, and produces the family movie for Imagine Entertainment.

name: G.O.B. Bluth
notes: Founder of the Alliance of Magicians. Illusions, not tricks ('A trick is something a whore does for money'). Segway rider. Chicken-dance performer (every Bluth's is wrong differently). Married a seal-and-knife saleswoman on a dare (never learns her name). Father of Steve Holt (with Eve Holt, high school). Later a born-again Christian magician, then closeted romance with rival magician Tony Wonder (s04–s05). Roofie circle inventor.

name: Lindsay Bluth Fünke
notes: Adopted by George Sr. and Lucille chiefly to spite Stan Sitwell; raised as Michael's twin. Married to Tobias Fünke; mother of Maeby. Serial causes: anti-war, anti-circumcision (HOOP), trees, wetlands ('the wetlands'). Runs for Congress s04–s05 ('Put up this wall!' flip). Discovers her adoption in s03 (Family Ties).

name: Buster Bluth
notes: Biological father is Oscar, not George Sr. Academic (cartography, 18th-century agrarian business); army stint. Left hand bitten off by a loose seal (s02e11 Out on a Limb) — hook, then prosthetics, then 'juicy' giant hand. Panic attacks; relationship with Lucille Austero ('Lucille 2'). Chief suspect in Lucille 2's disappearance (s05).

name: Tobias Fünke
notes: Former psychiatrist who lost his license (CPR on a man who wasn't having a heart attack). Aspiring actor; blue-paints himself for the Blue Man Group. Never-nude (cutoffs beneath everything; there are dozens of them). Poses as English housekeeper Mrs. Featherbottom to see Maeby. Catalog of unfortunate double entendres. Wrongly lands on an offender registry via paperwork mishap (s04); stages a Fantastic Four musical (s04–s05).

name: George Michael Bluth
notes: Runs the frozen banana stand ('Mr. Manager'). Longstanding crush on cousin Maeby (complicated by her adoption-adjacent status; they marry non-bindingly in s03 The Ocean Walker era and kiss atop the model home). Star student; UC Irvine. Invents the fake privacy software Fakeblock (s04) under alias George Maharis. Dates Ann Veal ('Her?') and later Rebel Alley (in unknowing rivalry with his father).

name: Maeby Fünke
notes: Conceived deliberately as a twist for a Lifetime-movie-style plan gone sideways; chronic schemer. Secretly a teenage studio executive at Tantamount Studios ('Marry me!'). Invents dying twin 'Surely' for sympathy fundraising. Repeatedly fails to graduate high school (still enrolled in s04). Wins an Opie award. In s05 poses as elderly 'Annette' to live free in a retirement community.

name: Oscar Bluth
relation: George Sr.'s identical twin; Buster's biological father
notes: Gentle, long-haired, pot-smoking songwriter ('All You Need Are Smiles'). Affair with Lucille; Buster is his son. Repeatedly imprisoned in George Sr.'s place — the twin swap is a running engine of the plot from s02 onward.

name: Tracey Bluth
relation: Michael's late wife; George Michael's mother
notes: Died of ovarian cancer before the series begins. Seen only in photographs and flashbacks; her absence shapes Michael and George Michael's father-son closeness.

name: Steve Holt
notes: Perpetual high-school senior and football star who announces his own name with both arms up. Learns G.O.B. is his father in s02 (The Immaculate Election arc); G.O.B. is slow to accept it. Later a pest-control businessman (s04–s05).

name: Annyong Bluth
relation: Adopted son (Korean adoptee; adopted to spite Buster)
notes: 'Annyong' is Korean for 'hello' — the family never learns his real name is Hel-loh. Adopted by Lucille in s01. Revealed in the s03 finale as a mole: grandson of Kim, whose banana stand idea George Sr. allegedly stole; his evidence sends Lucille toward justice.

name: Lucille Austero
relation: Lucille's best frenemy; Buster's on-off partner; Bluth Company stakeholder
notes: Vertigo-prone neighbor at Balboa Towers. Buys controlling interest in the Bluth Company. Runs for Congress against Lindsay. Disappears after Cinco de Cuatro (s04 finale); s05 centers on the mystery and the family's implication in it.
